# servicenow_group_role (Resource)

`servicenow_group_role` assigns a role to a user group within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Assigns a role to a group so all members inherit it.
resource "servicenow_group" "example" {
  name        = "Example Engineering Group"
  description = "Engineering team responsible for example services."
}

resource "servicenow_role" "example" {
  suffix      = "example_role"
  description = "Example role managed by Terraform."
}

resource "servicenow_group_role" "example" {
  group    = servicenow_group.example.id
  role     = servicenow_role.example.id
  inherits = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `group` (String) Sys ID of the user group record receiving the role assignment.
- `role` (String) Sys ID of the role record being assigned to the group.

### Optional

- `inherits` (Boolean) Whether members of the group inherit this role assignment.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_group_role.example <sys_id>
```
